summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

What is the definition of 'Independent events'?

"Independent events" means: Two events A and B are independent if P(A|B) = P(A), meaning knowing B occurred does not change the probability of A. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Dependent events'?

"Dependent events" means: Events where the occurrence of one changes the probability of the other; P(A|B) ≠ P(A). The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Multiplication rule for independents'?

"Multiplication rule for independents" means: P(A and B) = P(A) × P(B); valid only when A and B are independent events. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'At least one'?

""At least one" problems" means: P(at least one success in n independent trials) = 1 - P(no successes) = 1 - P(failure)^n. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Sampling with vs. without replacement'?

"Sampling with vs. without replacement" means: With replacement: draws are independent; without replacement: draws are dependent because the population changes after each draw. The other options describe different concepts.

summary_sections.title: Real-World Applications
summary_sections.content_html:
Real-World Applications

Example: Which of the following best defines "Multiplication rule for independents"?

"Multiplication rule for independents" is defined as: P(A and B) = P(A) × P(B); valid only when A and B are independent events. The other options describe different concepts.

Example: What is the definition of 'Multiplication rule for independents'?

"Multiplication rule for independents" means: P(A and B) = P(A) × P(B); valid only when A and B are independent events. The other options describe different concepts.
